title: Slow-cooker pulled pork
sources: US Southern barbecue tradition — pre-1928 published sources thin in public domain; method descended from West African enslaved-cook traditions adapted by white slaveholders, formalised through twentieth-century Southern cookbooks.
USDA / NCHFP material on pork cooking temperatures (~91°C / 195°F for shred).
notes: American slow-cooker classic. Pork shoulder (Boston butt) rubbed dry, cooked low and slow for 8-10 hours, shredded, dressed with a simple cider-vinegar sauce. The slow-cooker version is faithful to the Carolinas tradition. Batch-cook for the freezer. 8-10 hour total but ~25 min active. Serves 8 in buns; freezes well.
